subject: Physics
title: The Law of Inertia
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Law of Inertia.

hook.text: A hockey puck on perfect ice would slide forever. Newton's first law says nothing stops on its own.

buildup.text: Before Newton, people assumed moving objects naturally slow down. Friction fooled everyone.

discovery.text: Objects keep moving at constant speed unless a force acts on them. This is inertia.

twist.text: Galileo figured this out first. Newton gave him credit but got all the fame for writing it down.

climax.text: Inertia explains seatbelts, orbiting planets, and why coffee spills when your car brakes.

punchline.text: Objects in motion stay in motion. The universe is lazy.

quiz.question: What does Newton's first law (inertia) state?
options: Objects keep moving unless a force acts on them
Every action has an equal reaction
